{
  "claims": [
    "The California recall election system was designed in the early 20th century.",
    "The 2021 gubernatorial recall election raised questions about the adequacy of the current system's checks and balances for ensuring democratic and fair elections.",
    "California's existing recall process has been described as fundamentally undemocratic.",
    "California law allows for a candidate to be recalled and replaced by another candidate who receives far fewer votes.",
    "More than 70% of the attempts to recall elected state officials in California that have qualified for the ballot occurred in the last 27 years.",
    "Two statewide recall elections in California history have occurred.",
    "Each of California’s last nine Governors has faced multiple recall attempts, with only two qualifying for the ballot."
  ]
}
